actionable_feedback: Add a short validation_checks_log.txt (or validation_checks.md) listing the exact commands, code snippets, or shell/python commands used to compute each CSV column and the linear-fit parameters (intercept/slope). Include the exact numeric seeds, rounding rules, and any library versions used.

In safety_review.md add a single explicit tie-breaker policy sentence clarifying how directionality (disproportionality vs seriousness) and confounder score are used to override or escalate when abs(residual_points) is within the tolerance (e.g., 4.75-5.25 window). State if directionality/confounder takes precedence and by what priority.

Include a small reproducibility script (e.g., compute_priority.py or compute_priority.R) that reads the embedded rows, computes normalized components, priority_score, predicted_score, residuals, and writes pv_signal_priority.csv. Add unit assertions checking that R1..R4 match the documented CSV values within rounding tolerance.
Document the derivation of the 5-point residual tolerance from sensitivity_shift_pct=5 (explicit formula: sensitivity_shift_pct% of what scale) and confirm the 0-100 scaling assumption used.

Add a one-line note in the markdown acknowledging that artifact previews were truncated during automated grading and recommend a brief human review for clinical interpretation when decisions are high-stakes.
artifact_specific_comments: pv_signal_priority.csv: numeric columns (priority_score, predicted_score, residual_points) appear consistent with formulas in safety_review.md; include a validation checksum or included script invocation in the CSV metadata row.
safety_review.md: formulas and sensitivity analysis are present; add a short tie-breaker policy and a reproducibility snippet.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete, auditable, and reproducible from the embedded packet. Numeric values in pv_signal_priority.csv match the documented formulas in safety_review.md. The grader and verifier both awarded full credit. Recommended local improvements focus on traceable validation and an explicit tie-breaker policy for borderline residual cases (e.g., R3).
